figures.caption: Fig. 1: Worker W3 has failed. gi refers to the partial gradient on data subset Di. Based on work completed by W1 and W2, g1 + g2 + g3 can be computed.

Fig. 2: Gradient vectors are split into half [5], e.g., gi = [gi[0] T gi[1] T ]T . If all workers return their results, i.e., if there are no failures and worker Wi sends g̃i, the PS can calculate ∑3 i=1 gi[0] = g̃1 + g̃2, and ∑3 i=1 gi[1] = g̃2 + g̃3, i.e., the exact gradient.
Fig. 6: Training loss for a small neural network with different constructions, m = 2.
Fig. 4: Performance of the construction as in (7) for ϵ = .1, and m = 2 with a (27, 2, 5) coset bipartite graph.
Fig. 5: Performance of the construction as in (19) for a (40, 20, 3, 6) bi-regular bipartite graph and m = 2.
Fig. 3: Performance of the construction as in (7) for ϵ = 0, and m = 2 with a (91, 91, 10, 10, 1) BIBD.
